title: TOCTOU in SSRF guard: DNS resolved separately from HTTP request
description: Multiple locations (server.rs handle_inbox Follow handler, delivery.rs process_batch, actor_cache.rs, media.rs) check is_private_host_resolved() before making an HTTP request. The check resolves DNS and blocks private IPs, but the subsequent HTTP request resolves DNS independently. An attacker controlling a DNS server can return a public IP for the first resolution and a private IP (e.g., 169.254.169.254) for the second, bypassing the SSRF guard (DNS rebinding attack). The redirect policy is set to none which mitigates redirect-based SSRF, but the rebinding window remains. This is a known-hard problem; documenting it here as a tracked risk. Mitigation: use a custom DNS resolver that caches the resolved IP and connects to it directly, or use a connect-level callback to reject private IPs at socket time.
close_reason: Known TOCTOU limitation. Full fix requires custom DNS resolver in reqwest (hickory-dns). Current check is defense-in-depth with millisecond race window.
